channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vintage Photographs Showcase the Enduring Influence of Milliner Caroline Reboux on Mid-20th Century Fashion
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday celebrates the legacy of Caroline Reboux (1837–1927), one of the most influential milliners in fashion history. Known as the “Queen of Milliners,” Reboux shaped hat ...

item.title: Harriet Sperling and Peter Phillips wed in intimate ceremony attended by senior royals
item.description: Harriet Sperling, a 45-year-old paediatric nurse, married Peter Phillips, son of Princess Anne, in a private ceremony at All Saint's Church in Gloucestershire. The wedding was attended by senior membe...

item.title: Chip Stocks Tumble as Strong Jobs Data and Rate Fears Hit a Top-Heavy Market
item.description: Wall Street’s latest selloff hit technology especially hard, with semiconductor shares leading the decline after a stronger-than-expected May jobs report revived fears that the Federal Reserve may kee...

item.title: AI-generated videos and deepfakes influence Ontario politics
item.description: AI-generated content is becoming a notable factor in Ontario's political landscape. Alex Hout, an Ottawa-born creator now living in Switzerland, used AI tools to produce a satirical video called The G...

item.title: Opinion: Concerns over GOP handling of proposed ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’ and political loyalty to Trump
item.description: The article is an opinion column criticizing Republican lawmakers and former President Donald Trump over a proposed $1.8 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund,” which the author characterizes as a potentia...

item.title: US commits additional Ebola funding as CDC warns DRC outbreak could become one of the largest on record
item.description: The United States has announced an additional $38 million (about R629 million) in funding to support efforts to contain the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). This b...

item.title: Anambra Government Launches Free Statewide Hypertension Screening in Public Health Facilities
item.description: The Anambra State Government has started free hypertension screening services across all government-owned health facilities in the state as part of efforts to strengthen preventive healthcare and impr...

item.title: Authorities investigate 50-gallon stormwater spill at GKN Aerospace amid ongoing Orange County chemical incident
item.description: About 50 gallons of water spilled into a storm drain at the GKN Aerospace facility in Garden Grove, California, prompting new testing to determine whether the water contained any traces of toxic chemi...

item.title: A Personal Journey Through Rhodes: History, Family Roots and the Island’s Enduring Appeal
item.description: In this travel essay, Jim Bamboulis reflects on his deep personal connection to Rhodes, Greece, an island that has shaped his family history for generations. Although born in Canada, the author’s mate...

item.title: Chelle Defends Okoye After Nigeria’s 2-2 Draw Against Poland
item.description: Super Eagles coach Eric Chelle has come out to defend Maduka Okoye after Nigeria’s 2-2 friendly draw against Poland, saying the people criticising the goalkeeper did not properly watch the match. Acco...

item.title: Gold Star family frustrated as Defense Secretary Hegseth stops responding after initially promising help
item.description: A Gold Star family has expressed deep frustration after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth initially appeared willing to assist them in investigating the 2012 death of Navy SEAL Commander Job Price, only ...

item.title: Decline of House Sparrows in Urban India and Efforts to Restore Their Habitat
item.description: The article highlights the sharp decline of house sparrows across urban India and globally, pointing to changing city environments as the primary cause. Once a common presence in homes and neighbourho...

item.title: KMTC Student Eugene Mutuku Dies After Being Thrown from Nicco Sacco Matatu on Thika Road
item.description: A tragic incident has shocked many Kenyans after a young KMTC student, Eugene Mutuku, lost his life on Thika Road in Nairobi. The medical student had just started his attachment at a hospital in Kasar...

item.title: ANC supports government-led mediation in Pick n Pay retrenchment dispute impacting thousands of workers
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) Parliamentary Study Group on Employment and Labour has publicly supported Employment and Labour Minister Nomakhosazana Meth’s intervention in a major retrenchment d...

item.title: Karachi court sends suspect on two-day physical remand in wife murder case
item.description: A local court in Karachi has granted a two-day physical remand of a 63-year-old man accused of brutally killing his wife during a domestic dispute. The suspect, identified as Asghar Ali, was presented...

item.title: Two Municipal Traffic Enforcers Killed in Targeted Shooting While on Duty in Malabang, Lanao del Sur
item.description: Two municipal traffic enforcers were killed in a shooting incident in Malabang, Lanao del Sur, within the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM). The victims, identified as Samar Maro...

item.title: Death of former NHL player Claude Lemieux prompts renewed attention to CTE research after family donates brain
item.description: The death of former NHL player Claude Lemieux has prompted renewed attention to chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE) and the long-term impacts of repeated head trauma in contact sports. Lemieux, who...

item.title: Leading African Universities Recognised for International Collaboration and Academic Excellence
item.description: The article examines what sets certain African universities apart in the continent’s higher education landscape. It highlights four institutions that have distinguished themselves through strong inter...

item.title: Security concerns and possible protests raise tension ahead of Nairobi City Marathon
item.description: As Nairobi prepares to host the fifth edition of the Nairobi City Marathon, security agencies are raising concern over possible disruptions linked to ongoing public grievances across the country. Acco...

item.title: Duffy announces first live performance since revealing kidnapping and sexual assault experience
item.description: Welsh singer Duffy has announced that she will perform her first live concert in more than 15 years, marking a significant step in her return to public life and music. The artist, who achieved interna...
